title: get_time_context
description: Return the current time context for the active user: wall-clock time, day of week, time since last LLM prompt, length of the current session if one is open, and a heuristic energy zone bucket. No side effects; safe to call freely.

input.description: No inputs. This tool reads server-local state only.

now.description: Current wall-clock time as an ISO 8601 timestamp with an explicit timezone offset. Never naive.

day_of_week.description: Local day of the week, capitalised English.

time_since_last_prompt.description: ISO 8601 duration since the previous prompt was recorded by this server. PT0S if this is the first prompt of the process lifetime.

current_session_length.description: ISO 8601 duration since the current open session began. PT0S when no session is open.

energy_zone.description: Heuristic bucket for likely cognitive availability based on local clock-time and profile-declared rhythms. Skills MAY use this to shape output. Computation is documented in ADR 0001.

errors.description: Structured error codes this tool may return.

INTERNAL_CLOCK_UNAVAILABLE.description: The server could not read the system clock or timezone. Returned as a tool error, never as silent default.

PROFILE_UNREADABLE.description: Profile YAML present but unparseable; energy_zone falls back to 'unknown' rather than failing the whole call when possible.

examples.name: fresh process, no session, weekday morning

compatibility.policy: Additive-only within the v0.1.x line. New optional output fields MAY be appended; existing fields MUST NOT change type, name, or semantics. Any breaking change requires a new major version and a parallel schema $id.
compatibility.privacy: Reads local profile and local session state only. No remote calls. No data leaves the user's machine.
